title: How to delete traces or runs?

posts.author: joe
posts.body:
When a user deletes their account I want to delete all the user data on langsmith.
I have run ids stored in my db. How do I delete all traces/runs of that user based on these run ids?

posts.author: AbdulBasit
Isaac
posts.body:
There is a batch delete endpoint that you can access through the API
here

posts.author: mariana
posts.body: I tried using this and I get asked for a session_id that does not exist for my traces, why can’t I delete just using the trace_id?

posts.author: angus
posts.body:
Hi
@mariana
session_id is just the project ID for the trace you are trying to delete. You can find it on the tracer project page
Screenshot 2025-10-16 at 9.07.52 AM
1438×548 72 KB

posts.author: mariana
posts.body: thank you! I used session_id before, but it was related to threads

posts.author: mariana
posts.body:
Isaac:
here
Even though I have done this I get “Run deletes queued", but nothing gets deleted. I have no way to debug because even if I pass a wrong id it still says the same

posts.author: angus
posts.body:
docs.smith.langchain.com
Data Purging for Compliance | 🦜️🛠️ LangSmith
This guide covers the various features available after data reaches LangSmith Cloud servers to help you achieve your privacy goals.
Trace deletions are processed during non-peak usage times and are not instant, usually within a few hours. There is no confirmation of deletion - you’ll need to query the data again to verify it has been removed.
We process these deletions over the weekend now

posts.author: tefrati
posts.body:
Hello there,
Adding to what Mariana said above. The response status and messages are misleading. We’re getting “Run deletes queued” 202 response whether the metadata matched to actual traces or not. Same thing for trace_ids.
We need to have a better way to know if the request body matches actual traces in our projects. We also need a better way to know if traces were actually deleted. Asking us to check on Monday to see if it was deleted is not good enough. What if traces were not deleted? Should we try again, week after week?
Thank you

posts.author: benlammers
posts.body:
Jumping into this thread because also finding deleting traces to be a frustrating experience with the current API endpoints. Similar to tefrati’s comments it is difficult to have confidence that the data will be removed.
The documentation for these endpoints is also unclear as it incorrectly indicates to expect a 200 response and that data will be deleted typically “within a few hours” not just on weekends, also for /v1/runs/delete it is unclear to me if there is a limit to the number of runs that can be queued for deletion using a metadata filter.
ex. if I use metadata organization:x and that matches 1000s of runs, would they all be queued for deletion.
Clarification here would be greatly appreciated!
